evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR Android Activity I currently draw rectangle highlights. Switch to circle highlights instead — use the dot preset and make them pulse. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill changes the highlight provider to return a BarcodeArCircleHighlight built with the DOT preset and sets isPulsing = true, keeping the rest of the integration intact.

assertions.text: highlightForBarcode returns a BarcodeArCircleHighlight
BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset.DOT is passed to the BarcodeArCircleHighlight constructor
isPulsing is set to true on the circle highlight
BarcodeArRectangleHighlight is NOT returned from highlightForBarcode (semantic)
callback.onData( is used to deliver the highlight
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Customize the rectangle highlight in my MatrixScan AR Android app: give it a green brush and show a checkmark icon on top of each barcode. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the brush property of the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight to a green Brush and sets the icon property to a ScanditIcon built with ScanditIconBuilder using the checkmark icon type.

assertions.text: The brush property of the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight is assigned a Brush
The icon property of the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight is assigned a ScanditIcon
ScanditIconBuilder is used to build the icon
.withIcon( is called on the ScanditIconBuilder
ScanditIconType.CHECKMARK is used
.build() is called on the ScanditIconBuilder
callback.onData( is used to deliver the highlight

evals.prompt: Replace the info annotation in my MatrixScan AR Android Activity with a status icon annotation that shows a short status and is anchored above the barcode. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation from annotationForBarcode, sets its text and anchors it to the top via BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotationAnchor.TOP.

assertions.text: annotationForBarcode returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation
BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is constructed with context and barcode
The text property of the BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is set
BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotationAnchor.TOP is assigned to the anchor property
callback.onData( is used to deliver the annotation

evals.prompt: Make the info annotation in my MatrixScan AR Android app richer: add a header with the text 'Product', keep the barcode data as a body row, and add a footer that says 'Tap for details'. Use the medium width. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation with a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader, a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent for the barcode data, and a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationFooter, setting width to MEDIUM.

assertions.text: A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader is constructed and its text is set to Product
The header property of the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is assigned the BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationFooter is constructed and its text is set to Tap for details
The footer property of the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is assigned the BarcodeArInfoAnnotationFooter
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent holds the barcode data (barcode.data)
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationWidthPreset.MEDIUM is assigned to the width property
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.barcode.ar.ui.annotations.info
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationFooter is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.barcode.ar.ui.annotations.info

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR Android app I want a popover with action buttons on each barcode — one 'Pick' button — and I need to know which button was tapped. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation built with a list of BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton(icon, text) and wires a listener whose onPopoverButtonTapped receives the tapped button and index.

assertions.text: annotationForBarcode returns a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation
A BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton is constructed with an icon and a text label
The BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton list is passed to the BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation constructor
A listener is assigned to the BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation
onPopoverButtonTapped is overridden
The buttonIndex parameter is used to identify which button was tapped (semantic)
callback.onData( is used to deliver the annotation

evals.prompt: I want the annotation in my MatrixScan AR Android app to show a compact tooltip when the barcode is far away and a detailed one when it's close up. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation constructed with a close-up BarcodeArInfoAnnotation and a far-away BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, optionally tuning the static threshold.

assertions.text: annotationForBarcode returns a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation
A close-up BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is constructed
A far-away BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is constructed
Both BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances are passed to the BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation constructor
The close-up annotation is shown when the barcode area exceeds the threshold (semantic)
callback.onData( is used to deliver the annotation

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan AR Android info annotations pop up automatically as soon as a barcode is scanned. I only want them to appear when the user taps the highlight. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the annotationTrigger property of the info annotation to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HIGHLIGHT_TAP.

assertions.text: BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HIGHLIGHT_TAP is assigned to the annotationTrigger property
The annotationTrigger is set on the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation
Existing annotation provider code is preserved

evals.prompt: Show the built-in torch and camera-switch controls in my MatrixScan AR Android view. Put the torch control in the top-left and the camera switch in the top-right. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowTorchControl and shouldShowCameraSwitchControl to true on the BarcodeArView and assigns their positions via Anchor.

assertions.text: shouldShowTorchControl is set to true on the BarcodeArView
Anchor.TOP_LEFT is assigned to torchControlPosition
shouldShowCameraSwitchControl is set to true on the BarcodeArView
Anchor.TOP_RIGHT is assigned to cameraSwitchControlPosition
Anchor is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.core.common.geometry

evals.prompt: I need a fully custom highlight in my MatrixScan AR Android app — a custom View drawn over each barcode rather than the built-in rectangle or circle. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a class conforming to BarcodeArHighlight, overriding createView() to return the custom View and update(view, barcodeLocation) to position it, and returns it from highlightForBarcode.

assertions.text: A class implements the BarcodeArHighlight interface
createView() is overridden and returns a View
update is overridden with a View parameter and a Quadrilateral barcodeLocation parameter
The custom BarcodeArHighlight is delivered via callback.onData(
No BarcodeArCustomHighlight type is used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want a completely custom annotation View in my MatrixScan AR Android app instead of the built-in info or status annotations. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a class conforming to BarcodeArAnnotation, sets annotationTrigger, overrides createView() and update(barcodeLocation, highlightViewLocation, view), and returns it from annotationForBarcode.

assertions.text: A class implements the BarcodeArAnnotation interface
The annotationTrigger property is overridden with a BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger value
createView() is overridden and returns a View
update is overridden with a barcodeLocation parameter and a highlightViewLocation parameter and a view parameter
The custom BarcodeArAnnotation is delivered via callback.onData(
No BarcodeArCustomAnnotation type is used (semantic)
